title: NQ Manipulation Leg Context
focus_questions: Did this leg begin from a key position or from the middle of noise?
Was a real objective reached, or was the label applied too early?

summary.thesis: A manipulation leg matters when it starts from a key position, creates real displacement, and points toward a real objective that changes the later script.
summary.operator_translation: Do not label every fast move as manipulation; the origin and objective must be meaningful.
summary.ai_usage_note: Use this card to filter out fake manipulation labels and keep only the legs that actually changed inventory or expectation.
summary.ai_brief: Environment: a forcing move starting from a meaningful location. Required evidence: key origin, measurable displacement, and progress toward a primary or secondary objective. Do not trade: if the move begins in the middle of balance or the objective is already complete. Real trigger: a leg that changes the script away from origin. Failure point: the move collapses back into the origin without follow-through.

when_relevant: A fast leg begins from a meaningful location.
The move creates displacement large enough to matter.
Later behavior depends on whether that forcing move reached its objective.
required_evidence: the leg starts from a key position rather than the middle of balance
displacement from the origin is measurable
a primary or secondary objective can be stated and reviewed
confirmation_signals: the leg creates clean displacement away from origin
price reaches at least the primary objective and can still press toward the secondary objective
later participants are forced to react to the new displacement rather than ignore it
invalidation_signals: price quickly collapses back into the origin
the move cannot reach any meaningful objective
the supposed leg begins from noise and produces no script change
no_trade_conditions: Do not label every fast impulse as a manipulation leg when it begins in the middle of balance.
Do not chase a leg after most of its objective is already completed and the market is moving into exhaustion.
Do not use the leg as a reason to ignore a stronger opposite higher-timeframe context.

entry_archetypes.name: objective-led continuation
entry_archetypes.description: Trade after the leg proves it is still driving toward an uncompleted objective.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the market is already at the second objective and exhausting.

entry_archetypes.name: retest of manipulated origin
entry_archetypes.description: Trade the retest only if the origin still behaves like the causal level after displacement.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the origin is already being accepted through.

management_notes: Keep the origin, objective, and follow-through tied together.
A manipulation leg that fails early should be downgraded quickly.
This card is mostly a context filter, not a blind trigger generator.
review_questions: What key position made this leg meaningful before it started?
Did the move reach a real objective, or was the label applied too early?
After the leg completed, did the market distribute, continue, or fail straight back into the origin?
